prompt: Which Renaissance artist painted the Mona Lisa?
The Eiffel Tower was originally built as the entrance arch for which event?
Which two primary colours do you mix to make orange?
The famous sculpture of a man sitting and thinking, head resting on his fist, is called what?
Vincent van Gogh sold only ONE painting during his entire lifetime. What was it called?
The Art Nouveau movement got its name from a shop in which city?
Which Japanese art form — a centuries-old practice of folding paper into animals and geometric shapes — literally translates as "paper folding"?
The Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao, Spain — one of the most celebrated buildings of the 20th century — was designed by which architect?
Frida Kahlo painted many self-portraits because she spent long periods bedridden after a bus accident. What unusual feature did she include in almost all of them?
options: A mirror painted into the background
A pet monkey sitting on her shoulder
Flowers woven directly into her painted hair

prompt: The Pantone colour-matching system assigns numbers to specific hues. What colour is Pantone 448 C, officially voted the world's ugliest colour and used on cigarette packaging to deter smokers?
The ancient Egyptians used a blue pigment so prized that its formula was lost for centuries. What is it called?
Which radical design school, founded in Weimar, Germany in 1919, united fine art with craft and industrial production — and was shut down by the Nazis in 1933?
Which Dutch artist is famous for paintings of swirling skies and sunflowers, and for cutting off part of his own ear?
What do we call a painting of a bowl of fruit, flowers, or other objects that don't move?
Which famous melting-clocks painting was created by Salvador Dalí?
The ceiling of the Sistine Chapel, showing God reaching out to Adam, was painted by which artist?
What is the name of the flat board an artist holds to mix paint colours while working?
Andy Warhol made a famous pop-art print of cans of which everyday product?
On the artist's colour wheel, which colour sits directly opposite blue as its 'complementary' colour?
The Statue of Liberty was a gift to the United States from which country?
Edvard Munch's 'The Scream' shows a figure clutching its face on a bridge under a sky of which dominant colour?
Which movement, named after a Monet painting, was first mocked by critics as the work of unfinished 'impressions'?
In typography, what is the small decorative stroke at the ends of letters in fonts like Times New Roman called?
Pablo Picasso co-founded a movement that broke objects into geometric shapes seen from many angles at once. What was it called?
Which artist is best known for large 'drip paintings' made by flinging and pouring paint onto canvas laid on the floor?
The Terracotta Army — thousands of life-sized clay soldiers — was buried to guard the tomb of the first emperor of which country?
After Banksy's 'Girl with Balloon' shredded itself moments after selling at auction in 2018, the half-destroyed work was given a brand-new official title. What is it now called?
One 20th-century artist became so identified with a single deep shade that he patented his own version of it in 1960 and used it to paint blank monochrome canvases. Which artist?
In Japanese craft, 'kintsugi' repairs broken pottery by filling the cracks with lacquer dusted with which precious metal?
The RGB colour model used by screens is 'additive' — what colour do you get when red, green, and blue light are combined at full strength?
Artemisia Gentileschi was a celebrated Baroque painter known for dramatic biblical scenes like 'Judith Slaying Holofernes'. In which century did she work?
Grant Wood's 'American Gothic' shows a stern farmer holding which tool beside a woman in front of a house?
